_meta.description: Complete E2E test plan for the AppsFlyer Flutter plugin. Covers SDK initialization, pre/post-start API calls, in-app event HTTP validation, callback verification, and deep link scenarios (background and foreground) — on both Android and iOS.

phases.name: Smoke — SDK Init, APIs, Events, HTTP
phases.description: Fresh install. Validate SDK startup sequence, all pre/post-start API calls return expected values, three auto-launched events fire and reach the server with 200 OK, and all standard callbacks fire correctly.

fresh_install.description: App was uninstalled and reinstalled from scratch

pre_start_apis.description: All pre-start API calls complete without error

post_start_apis.description: getVersionNumber and getSDKVersion return non-empty values

event_af_demo_launch.description: af_demo_launch event fires and gets HTTP 200

event_af_purchase.description: af_purchase event fires and gets HTTP 200

event_af_content_view.description: af_content_view event fires and gets HTTP 200

no_fatal_errors.description: No fatal exceptions or SDK start errors in logs

phases.name: Background Deep Link
phases.description: App is backgrounded after SDK start (home press on Android, Safari on iOS). Deep link URL is triggered and brings app back to foreground. onDeepLinking fires with Status.FOUND. Can reuse the same app session from Phase 1.
phases.note: Run immediately after Phase 1 completes — app is still running. No reinstall needed.

launch_http_200.description: LAUNCH HTTP event sent with af_deeplink and returns 200

no_not_found_after_deeplink.description: No Status.NOT_FOUND after the deep link was triggered
no_not_found_after_deeplink.note: iOS fires NOT_FOUND on initial launch only — only check logs AFTER the deep link trigger timestamp

phases.name: Foreground Deep Link
phases.description: Fresh install. App is in foreground after SDK start. A brief launcher/settings switch triggers onPause (required for SDK to re-process intent on resume). Deep link brings app back. onDeepLinking fires with Status.FOUND.
phases.note: Requires a second fresh install. The onPause/onResume cycle is mandatory — direct onNewIntent without prior onPause does NOT trigger onDeepLinking on either platform.

android: confirm app is in foreground: adb shell dumpsys activity activities | grep mResumedActivity | grep appsflyer
briefly switch to launcher: adb shell am start -a android.intent.action.MAIN -c android.intent.category.HOME
trigger deep link: adb shell am start -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d "afexample://deeplink?deep_link_value=qa_deeplink_fg&af_sub1=foreground_test&pid=testmedia&c=deeplink_test"
ios: briefly switch to Settings: xcrun simctl launch <UDID> com.apple.Preferences
trigger deep link: xcrun simctl openurl <UDID> "afexample://deeplink?deep_link_value=qa_deeplink_fg&af_sub1=foreground_test&pid=testmedia&c=deeplink_test"

is_first_launch_true_before_deeplink.description: Confirm onInstallConversionData fired with is_first_launch=true before triggering deep link

launch_http_200.description: LAUNCH HTTP event with af_deeplink returns 200

execution_order.description: Run Phase 1 first on a platform. Phase 2 immediately follows using the same running app. Phase 3 requires a new fresh install.
execution_order.phase_1_to_phase_2: no_reinstall_needed — app is still running after Phase 1

report_schema.description: Schema for the JSON report file saved after each full plan run

fields._meta: run_date, plan_version, overall_status, platforms_run, total_checks, passed, failed, warned
fields.results: array of phase+platform result objects, each with: phase_id, platform, device, status, checks (map of check_id to {status, evidence, note}), captured_logs
fields.summary_table: flat map of phase+platform to PASS|FAIL|SKIP
fields.comparison_hint: baseline versions used — useful for spotting baseline drift
